methodsAttempted.query: site:ufs.ac.za University of the Free State artificial intelligence policy generative AI ChatGPT academic integrity
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found an official UFS AI Position Statement PDF, official UFS news about AI detection and academic integrity, an official lecture news item, and UFS-hosted journal material.

methodsAttempted.query: site:ufs.ac.za/docs/default-source UFS AI Position Statement 2025 PDF
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search confirmed the official AI Position Statement PDF and found adjacent strategic/learning-and-teaching documents with lower direct policy specificity.

methodsAttempted.query: site:ufs.ac.za AI detection software Turnitin generative AI UFS July 2026
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search confirmed the official UFS news item announcing discontinuation of AI detection software from 1 July 2026.

methodsAttempted.query: site:ufs.ac.za generative AI guidelines students staff UFS academic integrity attribution transparency
methodsAttempted.notes: Search returned the same official academic-integrity news item plus UFS-hosted journal articles. The journal articles were not used as UFS policy evidence.
Direct checks for /robots.txt and /sitemap.xml returned the UFS 404 page rather than machine-readable files; no robots disallow rule was found from those public paths.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found a Senate-approved University of the Free State AI Position Statement PDF and an official UFS news item announcing the institution's July 2026 AI-detection change. Event coverage, adjacent strategy material, and UFS-hosted journal scholarship were documented but rejected as lower-specificity or non-policy evidence.

stopConditions: Stop after official UFS sources support narrow AI policy and academic-integrity claim candidates.

Keep source evidence snippets short and tied to official URLs.

artifacts.sourceTitle: University of the Free State AI Position Statement
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Academic rigour, integrity and honesty: In embracing the potentials of AI, it is imperative to uphold the fundamental principles of academic integrity and ethical conduct in higher education. artifacts.sourceTitle: UFS shifts academic integrity approach in AI era
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The University of the Free State (UFS) will discontinue the use of AI detection software across all faculties and academic activities from 1 July 2026, signalling a major shift in how the institution approaches academic integrity in an increasingly AI-enabled higher education environment.

artifacts.sourceTitle: UFS shifts academic integrity approach in AI era
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: For students, the shift means that AI detection tools will no longer form part of assessment or academic integrity processes. At the same time, the university reaffirmed that students remain responsible for producing original work and for using generative AI tools honestly and appropriately within institutional guidelines.

artifacts.title: University of the Free State AI Policy Source Update
artifacts.summary: Official UFS sources include a Senate-approved AI Position Statement covering generative and assistive AI, academic integrity, privacy/data security, and AI literacy support, plus a 2026 official UFS news item announcing discontinuation of AI detection software from 1 July 2026.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The AI Position Statement is a position statement and guiding-principles source; it should not be expanded into detailed operational rules beyond the cited language.
The AI detection claim is based on an official UFS news announcement dated 21 May 2026 and should be checked again after the 1 July 2026 effective date.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
